title: Architecture Highlights
intro: Selected systems and integration problems where I had to think beyond feature delivery, with a focus on payment flows, asynchronous correctness, tenant isolation, and recovery design.

items.title: Multi-Tenant Regional Payment Orchestration
items.context: Designed a billing orchestration approach for Shopify stores that needed regional payment methods beyond Shopify's standard flow.
items.constraint: Different markets, provider behaviors, asynchronous callbacks, duplicate or out-of-order events, and tenant-specific credentials made billing correctness the real challenge.
items.decision: Separated the system into tenant-aware routing, provider adapters, durable webhook ingestion, explicit billing state transitions, and reconciliation workers.
items.impact: Turned provider-specific volatility into a controlled orchestration problem, reducing the risk of double-processing, missing payment state, and cross-tenant blast radius.

items.title: Regional Payment Integrations for Shopify
items.context: Built regional payment solutions including ETH/Web3 checkout, GMO virtual account transfer, and subscription billing flows not fully covered by Shopify-native capabilities.
items.constraint: The systems had to work across multiple stores and markets, support async payment confirmation, and keep order state aligned with external provider truth.
items.decision: Used middleware-style app services with signed callbacks, background workers, cron-based recovery, and explicit lifecycle handling for payment, cancel, resume, refund, and expiry flows.
items.impact: This required architecture decisions around reliability, state ownership, and operational recovery in real commerce environments, not just gateway integration.

items.title: Durable Webhook Inbox and Replay
items.context: Designed a compact webhook processing pattern for systems where external providers deliver events at least once and on their own retry schedule.
items.constraint: Slow downstream dependencies, duplicate deliveries, per-resource ordering, and failure recovery can easily break correctness if webhook handling is synchronous.
items.decision: Applied an inbox-first pattern: accept fast, persist first, process asynchronously, protect side effects with idempotency, and allow intentional replay for failed events.
items.impact: The pattern is directly relevant to payment and commerce systems where reliability depends more on recovery design than on the happy path.
